Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for Elena's email to Samira about cost infrastructure status. Tool: search_emails with query "cost estimator" or "CloudWatch" or "Q1 allocation". Finds email_elena_cost_infra_status_001 from elena.rostova@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com asking about three items: the infrastructure cost estimator tool, CloudWatch cost alert thresholds (50%, 100%, 200% with Slack notification to finance), and the per-client AWS cost breakdown ($42,870 unallocated) blocking Q1 close. Elena references Driftwood being close to a proposal and demands answers by end of week.
- Search Slack #engineering (C003) for CloudWatch, cost alert, or budget tagging updates from Lena. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "CloudWatch" or "budget tagging" or "cost alert" in channel C003. Finds Lena's status message (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, ts 1776942000, April 22) reporting: 50% and 100% CloudWatch alarms active on EC2, 200% alarm configured but pointing at a test SNS topic not the production one that posts to finance, Lambda function for daily Cost Explorer checks not deployed (pulled onto DOT compliance), budget tagging done for EC2 and S3 by customer ID but RDS not tagged.
- Search Slack #engineering (C003) for the original cost infrastructure task assignment. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "cost estimator" or "P0" or "P1" in channel C003. Finds Samira's own prioritization post (ts 1775059936, April 1) listing P0 items (cost alerts, budget tags), P1 item (infrastructure cost estimator tool with 2-week ETA), and P2 items (autoscaling/spot instance review, S3 lifecycle policies). Confirms the cost estimator was assigned as P1 but never formalized into a Linear ticket.
- Search Linear for any existing ticket related to the infrastructure cost estimator tool. Tool: linear_list_issues with query "cost estimator" or "infrastructure cost" scoped to team_engineering. Returns no matching ticket, confirming the cost estimator was never turned into a Linear issue and is still living as an undocumented Slack ask.
- Search Slack #finance (C005) for cost allocation, per-client breakdown, or Q1 close discussions from Marcus. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "allocation" or "Q1" or "42,870" in channel C005. Finds Marcus's message (moveops_marcus_thorne, ts 1777014000, April 23) flagging that Q1 close is overdue, the $42,870 AWS bill is still a single unallocated line item, he needs the per-client breakdown before Alejandro can post journal entries, and he questions whether the Vantage contract covers the variable compute costs. Message has reply_count 1.
- Retrieve the thread reply on Marcus's #finance message. Tool: conversations_replies with channel_id C005 and thread_ts "1777014000.000000". Finds Alejandro's reply (moveops_alejandro_fuentes, ts 1777019400, April 23) revealing that the Vantage setup fee invoice NMD-2026-0042 for $462,500 explicitly covers platform access and onboarding services only, variable compute costs above the $14,000/month base allocation are excluded, the March AWS overage of $28,870 is unrecoverable under the current contract unless renegotiated with Rachel, and this is a contract gap not a billing error.
- Search Slack #customer-engagement (C002) for sales infrastructure sizing or prospect discussions. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "cost model" or "infrastructure" or "proposal" in channel C002. Finds Julian's message (moveops_julian_brooks, ts 1776841200, April 21) about Driftwood Analytics moving faster than expected, wanting to onboard 50 employees in June, needing a formal proposal by May 5, and requesting a per-employee cost model from engineering before he can put numbers on paper. Julian explicitly references not wanting another Vantage situation.
- Search emails for the original AWS cost spike RCA and Elena's directives to get full context on what was ordered. Tool: search_emails with query "AWS" or "cost spike" or "infrastructure". Finds Samira's RCA email to Marcus and Elena (line 3447) with the $42,870 breakdown (EC2 $28,450, S3 $5,500, RDS $8,920) and Elena's directive email (line 3451) ordering cost alert implementation, cost estimator prioritization, and a 500-employee engineering sign-off threshold.
- Look up Elena Rostova's contact details for the reply email. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Elena Rostova". Finds elena.rostova@moveops.com.
- Create a Linear ticket for the infrastructure cost estimator tool. Tool: linear_create_issue with team: team_engineering, title referencing the cost estimator tool, description including Elena's directive, Julian's Driftwood deadline (May 5), and the need for a per-employee AWS cost model. Set priority: 1 (Urgent) or 2 (High).
- Update existing engineering tickets or create additional tickets for the CloudWatch alert completion work and RDS budget tagging work so the Linear board reflects the real status. Tool: linear_create_issue or linear_update_issue. If creating new tickets, one for completing CloudWatch alerts (connect 200% alarm to production SNS, deploy Lambda) and one for RDS tagging plus Q1 cost allocation delivery, both assigned to Lena with urgent priority.
- Send email to elena.rostova@moveops.com from samira.tariq@moveops.com with engineering status on all three items she asked about: CloudWatch alerts partially deployed with specific gaps (200% alarm on test SNS, no Lambda, no RDS tags), cost estimator tool never ticketed and now formalized with a deadline, and Q1 cost allocation blocked by incomplete RDS tagging. Tool: send_email.
- Post a message in #engineering (C003) announcing the reprioritization of cost infrastructure work, including ticket references, deadlines, and direction for the team. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel_id C003.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
